item.title: Leukemia WARNING: 7% donor crisis
item.description: At just 24 years old, Caitlin Leggett's life was upended by an acute myeloid leukemia (AML) diagnosis. When doctors prepared her sister Grace for a life-saving stem cell transplant, they made a shocking discovery: the sisters were identical twins, making Grace's stem cells unusable. Now facing a terminal six-month prognosis and a severe national donor shortage—with only 7% of the eligible UK population registered—Caitlin is in a race against time. This episode explores Caitlin's resilient fight, the critical importance of the stem cell registry, and her family's urgent 'Saving Caitlin' campaign to raise £500,000 for specialized overseas treatment.
item.dc:creator: ACDT
item.itunes:summary: At just 24 years old, Caitlin Leggett's life was upended by an acute myeloid leukemia (AML) diagnosis. When doctors prepared her sister Grace for a life-saving stem cell transplant, they made a shocking discovery: the sisters were identical twins, making Grace's stem cells unusable. Now facing a terminal six-month prognosis and a severe national donor shortage—with only 7% of the eligible UK population registered—Caitlin is in a race against time. This episode explores Caitlin's resilient fight, the critical importance of the stem cell registry, and her family's urgent 'Saving Caitlin' campaign to raise £500,000 for specialized overseas treatment.
